text.title: rust-testing-ci-toolchain Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the rust-testing-ci-toolchain methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Use cargo nextest run in CI instead of cargo test. Configure .config/nextest.toml with fail-fast = false, retries = 1 on the ci profile, and a slow-timeout to catch hangs.
rule.rationale: cargo test runs tests sequentially within a binary with no per-test timeout. A single hanging test blocks the suite silently.

rule.statement: Upload cargo llvm-cov --lcov output to Codecov or Coveralls on every PR. Do not enforce a coverage percentage gate in CI.
rule.rationale: Hard coverage gates incentivize trivial tests; soft signals keep reviewers informed without gamification.

rule.statement: Run cargo +nightly miri test on every PR for crates that contain unsafe or FFI. Gate miri on its own CI job (it is slow); do not block the main test job on it.
rule.rationale: Miri catches UB the borrow checker cannot — pointer offsets, uninitialized reads, data races in unsafe Send/Sync.

rule.statement: Pin cargo nextest, cargo llvm-cov, and cargo miri versions via taiki-e/install-action and manage updates with Renovate. Never use @latest in CI tool installs.
rule.rationale: Floating @latest breaks CI silently when a new release changes behavior or output.

rule.statement: Configure a CI build cache for ~/.cargo/registry, ~/.cargo/git, and target/ keyed on toolchain + Cargo.lock hash.
rule.rationale: Cold builds of test binaries cost 3-5 min; warm cache cuts to 20-30 sec.

rule.statement: Skip when the suite is trivially small or covered by an existing CI pipeline that already enforces r1-r5.
rule.rationale: Prevents duplicate work when the project does not need this methodology.
